messages.content: Now reviewing all stories together for integration and end-to-end test coverage...

Cross-story integration and E2E tests are needed to validate unified generic failures, lockout + disabled interactions, remember-me TTL behaviors, and session invalidation across both stories. Notable inconsistencies (generic error text and remember-me TTL) must be resolved or made config-driven.

Defined cross-story integration and E2E tests focusing on unified error semantics, lockout + disabled/invited interactions, Remember me TTL behavior (conflicting 30 vs 180 days), session revocation on disablement, cookie security flags, and race-safety. Several spec conflicts surfaced and are listed as open questions.

Cross-story integration and E2E tests are needed to validate unified 401 behavior, lockout + disabled/invited interactions, Remember me TTL and sliding refresh, and immediate session invalidation on disablement across both stories. There are copy/TTL configuration conflicts to resolve.

Q: Generic error message string differs between stories ('We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password.' vs 'Invalid email or password.'). Which exact copy is canonical for all 401s?
A: We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password

Q: Remember me inactivity TTL conflict: Story 1 uses 30 days; Story 2 uses 180 days (rolling). What is the final TTL value and sliding policy? Should standard 8h sessions also be sliding?
A: 180 days (rolling)

Q: Configuration source of truth: Should there be a single env var for Remember me TTL (e.g., AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS) used across both stories? If both AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS and REMEMBER_ME_IDLE_TTL_DAYS exist, which wins?
A: Use AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS

Q: Do failed attempts for disabled or invited users count toward the per-account lockout threshold (Story 1 says yes; Story 2 is silent)?
A: Yes

Q: While an account is locked, should subsequent attempts increment failed_signin_count or only log LOCKED_OUT without changing counters?
A: only log LOCKED_OUT without changing counters

Q: Upon disabling a user, should we proactively invalidate all sessions immediately (push) or only 'on next request' as Story 2 states? If proactive invalidation exists, how is it triggered?
A:  'on next request'

Q: After an account transitions from locked to disabled and later back to active, should prior lockout_until remain in effect or be cleared on status change to active?
A: be cleared on status change to active

Q: Should UI display any specific messaging when a session is invalidated due to disablement (e.g., a banner) or always fall back to the same generic error upon next login attempt?
A: always fall back to the same generic error upon next login attempt

Q: SameSite policy value: Lax or Strict? Should this be consistent across both session modes and documented for cross-site SSO considerations (even if SSO is out of scope)?
A: Lax, be consistent

Q: Required logging/metrics schema: finalize reason enums, levels (INFO/WARN), and PII policy (log normalized email vs userId) so tests assert the correct shape without leaking sensitive data.
A: Sounds good

Integration and E2E tests updated per clarified decisions: canonical generic copy, 180-day rolling Remember me via AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS, SameSite=Lax, lockout rules, and on-next-request disablement invalidation.

Q: Should the standard 8-hour session be sliding as well, or remain non-sliding (only Remember me is rolling)? The answer specified 180-day rolling for Remember me but did not explicitly confirm standard session behavior.
A: Sliding

Q: For logging/metrics, finalize exact fields, enum values, and levels (e.g., INFO for SUCCESS, WARN for failures) and whether to log normalized email, userId, or both under the PII policy so assertions can target precise shapes.
A: You decide a minimum set of logging/metrics - keep it light for now

Updated integration and E2E tests reflect standard session as sliding and define a minimal logging schema, while preserving canonical error copy, 180-day rolling Remember me via AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS, SameSite=Lax, and on-next-request disablement invalidation.

latestTestPlannerResponse.message: Updated integration and E2E tests reflect standard session as sliding and define a minimal logging schema, while preserving canonical error copy, 180-day rolling Remember me via AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS, SameSite=Lax, and on-next-request disablement invalidation.

testPlan.title: Integration: 401 payload uniformity across failure modes with canonical copy
testPlan.description: Given attempts with unknown email, wrong password, disabled, invited, and locked-out accounts, when calling POST /auth/login, then API responds with HTTP 401, identical payload, and message 'We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password' for all cases; no reason-specific fields leak.

testPlan.title: Integration: Remember me TTL and env precedence
testPlan.description: Given AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS is set (e.g., 180), when logging in with rememberMe=true, then the issued cookie Max-Age and server-side TTL reflect 180 days; if REMEMBER_ME_IDLE_TTL_DAYS is also set, AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS takes precedence.

testPlan.title: Integration: Sliding inactivity refresh for both session modes
testPlan.description: Given authenticated sessions for rememberMe=true and rememberMe=false, when making periodic authenticated requests within their respective TTLs, then both sessions extend their expiry (sliding window); after inactivity beyond TTL, the next request returns 401 and the cookie is cleared.

testPlan.title: Integration: Standard session non-persistent cookie with security flags
testPlan.description: Given login with rememberMe=false, then the session cookie is a session cookie (no Max-Age/Expires), has Secure and HttpOnly set, SameSite=Lax, and no auth tokens appear in localStorage/sessionStorage.

testPlan.title: Integration: Disablement revokes active sessions on next request
testPlan.description: Given a user with active sessions (remember and standard), when the user is disabled and then each session makes the next authenticated request, then API returns 401 with canonical copy and sets the cookie to expire (Max-Age=0); no proactive revocation occurs before the next request.

testPlan.title: Integration: Failures for disabled/invited increment counters and can lock out
testPlan.description: Given a disabled or invited user, when repeated sign-in attempts occur within the lockout window, then failed_signin_count increments and the account transitions to locked-out after the threshold; responses remain generic 401.

testPlan.title: Integration: Locked-out attempts do not increment counters and skip password verification
testPlan.description: Given lockout_until > now, when attempting sign-in, then password verification is bypassed, failed_signin_count is unchanged, reason=LOCKED_OUT is logged, and the response is the canonical generic 401.

testPlan.title: Integration: Unknown email uses dummy constant-time verify
testPlan.description: Given a login attempt with an unknown email, then a dummy constant-time verify path runs, no user rows are created/updated, logs show reason=UNKNOWN_EMAIL, and latency distribution is comparable to wrong-password for an existing user.

testPlan.title: Integration: Concurrency at threshold yields a single lockout transition
testPlan.description: Given parallel bad-password attempts near the threshold for one account, when executed concurrently, then exactly one lockout state transition occurs, counters remain consistent, and all responses are generic 401.

testPlan.title: Integration: Minimal auth logging schema without client leakage
testPlan.description: Across SUCCESS, UNKNOWN_EMAIL, BAD_PASSWORD, STATUS_NOT_ELIGIBLE, and LOCKED_OUT, logs emit a minimal schema: level (INFO for SUCCESS, WARN for failures), event='auth.login', reason enum, userId when known (omit for unknown email), attemptCount for failures, lockoutUntil when locked, and requestId; no email or PII beyond userId is logged; client responses never include these internals.

testPlan.title: Integration: Lockout then disable then reactivate clears lockout
testPlan.description: Given a user is locked out and then disabled, when the user is reactivated (status active), then lockout_until and failed_signin_count are cleared, and a correct-credential login immediately succeeds.

testPlan.title: Integration: Auto-unlock on lockout expiry requires no background job
testPlan.description: Given an account locked with lockout_until in the past, when attempting sign-in, then the request is evaluated normally without background unlock; counters/window resume correctly and failures after unlock start a fresh window.

testPlan.title: Integration: Cookie security flags and SameSite=Lax for both modes
testPlan.description: After login with rememberMe true/false, verify session cookies set Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Lax; no auth artifacts present in localStorage/sessionStorage.

testPlan.title: E2E: Lockout → Remember me → disablement (next-request revocation)
testPlan.description: In the browser, fail login until lockout is triggered and confirm canonical generic 401. After lockout expiry, log in successfully with Remember me; restart the browser and confirm session persists. Then an admin disables the user; on the next navigation/API call, the app is logged out (401), redirected to /login, and subsequent login failures show the canonical generic copy.

testPlan.title: E2E: Invited user failures cause lockout; activation clears lockout
testPlan.description: As an invited user, attempt sign-in repeatedly to reach lockout (generic 401s). Admin activates the account; immediately sign in successfully (lockout cleared on activation) and access the app.

testPlan.title: E2E: Multi-device session revocation on disablement (next request)
testPlan.description: Sign in on Browser A with Remember me and on Browser B with a standard session. After admin disables the account, the first subsequent action on each browser yields 401, clears cookies, and both sessions are logged out.

testPlan.title: E2E: Generic error copy uniform across all failures
testPlan.description: From the login UI, attempt: wrong password, unknown email, disabled, invited, and locked. Each attempt displays exactly: 'We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password' with no additional hints or countdowns.

testPlan.title: E2E: Remember me persistence and sliding TTL with short test TTL
testPlan.description: With a short AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS (test-config), log in with Remember me; confirm persistence across browser restart and that periodic activity extends expiration; after inactivity beyond TTL, verify forced re-login with canonical copy.

testPlan.title: E2E: Standard session sliding inactivity expiry (short test TTL) and non-persistence across restart
testPlan.description: With a short standard TTL in test config, log in without Remember me; verify that periodic activity extends session while the browser remains open; after inactivity beyond TTL, the next action forces re-login with canonical copy; closing and reopening the browser drops the session regardless of inactivity.

testPlan.title: E2E: Cookie security flags visible in browser
testPlan.description: After logging in with and without Remember me, inspect cookies to confirm Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Lax are set and that no tokens are accessible via document.cookie or web storage.

teAnswers.56a22348-1aee-49a9-b581-c24e9bd2fcc6: We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password
teAnswers.a9ce3ca9-e80a-472e-9895-595ef1454838: only log LOCKED_OUT without changing counters
teAnswers.585eb3eb-8c09-4ece-a1d0-278ca75c72a6: be cleared on status change to active
teAnswers.7cca1d4d-671b-42d5-a357-36a569caa77a: always fall back to the same generic error upon next login attempt
teAnswers.ff028815-508e-415e-a44e-dbb577d5b184: You decide a minimum set of logging/metrics - keep it light for now
